methods.description: Process for preparing and analyzing tree-ring samples from various high-elevation and high-latitude sites to study historical climate conditions.

steps.description: We compiled 106 absolutely dated wood samples from living and relict trees that grew at 23 high-elevation and high-latitude sites in both hemispheres.
steps.searchable_summary: compile wood samples from various sites

steps.description: After cross-dating and quality control of each radial stem piece, rice starch was used to stabilise cell walls before thin sections were cut with a WSL-lab microtome.
steps.searchable_summary: stabilize and cut thin sections of tree samples

steps.description: The resulting 15 lm thin sections were bleached with sodium/potassium hypochlorite to decolour cell walls and remove undesired cell content.
steps.searchable_summary: bleach thin sections to decolour cell walls

steps.description: A 1:4 blend of Safranin and Astra Blue was then used to stain lignified cell walls red and less-lignified cell walls blue.
steps.searchable_summary: stain cell walls with Safranin and Astra Blue

steps.description: The resulting 106 double-stained thin sections were repeatedly rinsed with water and ethanol at increasing concentrations, and finally embedded in glycerol under cover glasses.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse and embed stained sections in glycerol

steps.description: A high-magnification compound microscope was used to record the occurrence of BRs, FRs, and LRs, and produce high-resolution images.
steps.searchable_summary: record and image cell structures using a microscope

steps.description: Ring widths between 530 and 550 CE were then measured on the same thin sections.
steps.searchable_summary: measure ring widths on thin sections
